children.heading: Liquidation by Board; bond; appointment of agent; fees to be fixed by Board
children.label: a Liquidation by Board; bond; appointment of agent; fees to be fixed by Board
A
children.text: Upon its finding that a Federal credit union insured under this subchapter is bankrupt or insolvent, the Board shall close such credit union for liquidation and appoint itself liquidating agent therefor.

children.label: B
children.text: Not later than 10 days after the date on which the Board closes a credit union for liquidation pursuant to paragraph (1), or accepts appointment as liquidating agent pursuant to subsection (b), such insured credit union may apply to the United States district court for the judicial district in which the principal office of such insured credit union is located or the United States District Court for the District of Columbia, for an order requiring the Board to show cause why it should not be prohibited from continuing such liquidation. Except as otherwise provided in this subparagraph, no court may take any action for or toward the removal of any liquidating agent or, except at the instance of the Board, restrain or affect the exercise of powers or functions of a liquidating agent.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Board as liquidating agent of a closed Federal credit union insured under this subchapter shall not be required to furnish bond and shall have the right to appoint an agent or agents to assist it in its duties as such liquidating agent. All fees, compensation, and expenses of liquidation and administration thereof shall be fixed by the Board and may be paid by them out of funds coming into its possession as such liquidating agent.

children.label: i
children.text: the credit union is significantly undercapitalized, as defined in , and has no reasonable prospect of becoming adequately capitalized, as defined in ; orsection 1790d of this titlesection 1790d of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the credit union is critically undercapitalized, as defined in ; andsection 1790d of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a State-chartered insured credit union, the Board has complied with section 1790d() of this title.l

children.heading: Powers and duties of Board as conservator or liquidating agent
children.label: b Powers and duties of Board as conservator or liquidating agent

children.heading: Rulemaking authority of Board
children.label: 1 Rulemaking authority of Board
children.text: The Board may prescribe such regulations as the Board determines to be appropriate regarding the conduct of the Board as conservator or liquidating agent.

children.label: i
children.text: all rights, titles, powers, and privileges of the credit union, and of any member, accountholder, officer, or director of such credit union with respect to the credit union and the assets of the credit union; and

children.label: ii
children.text: title to the books, records, and assets of any previous conservator or other legal custodian of such credit union.

children.label: i
children.text: take over the assets of and operate the credit union with all the powers of the members or shareholders, the directors, and the officers of the credit union and shall be authorized to conduct all business of the credit union;

children.label: ii
children.text: collect all obligations and money due the credit union;

children.label: iii
children.text: perform all functions of the credit union in the name of the credit union which is consistent with the appointment as conservator or liquidating agent; and

children.label: iv
children.text: preserve and conserve the assets and property of such credit union.

children.heading: Functions of credit union’s officers, directors, and shareholders
children.label: C Functions of credit union’s officers, directors, and shareholders
children.text: The Board may, by regulation or order, provide for the exercise of any function by any member or stockholder, director, or officer of any credit union for which the Board has been appointed conservator or liquidating agent.

children.label: i
children.text: necessary to put the credit union in a sound and solvent condition; and

children.label: ii
children.text: appropriate to carry on the business of the credit union and preserve and conserve the assets and property of the credit union.

children.heading: Additional powers as liquidating agent
children.label: E Additional powers as liquidating agent
children.text: The Board may, as liquidating agent, place the credit union in liquidation and proceed to realize upon the assets of the credit union, having due regard to the conditions of credit in the locality.

children.heading: Payment of valid obligations
children.label: F Payment of valid obligations
children.text: The Board, as conservator or liquidating agent, shall pay all valid obligations of the credit union in accordance with the prescriptions and limitations of this chapter.

children.heading: Attachment of assets and injunctive relief
children.label: G Attachment of assets and injunctive relief
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (H), any court of competent jurisdiction may, at the request of the Board (in the Board’s capacity as conservator or liquidating agent for any insured credit union or in the Board’s corporate capacity in the exercise of any authority under this section), issue an order in accordance with Rule 65 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, including an order placing the assets of any person designated by the Board under the control of the court and appointing a trustee to hold such assets.

children.heading: Showing
children.label: i Showing
children.text: Rule 65 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure shall apply with respect to any proceeding under subparagraph (G) without regard to the requirement of such rule that the applicant show that the injury, loss, or damage is irreparable and immediate.

children.heading: State proceeding
children.label: ii State proceeding
children.text: If, in the case of any proceeding in a State court, the court determines that rules of civil procedure available under the laws of such State provide substantially similar protections to such party’s right to due process as Rule 65 (as modified with respect to such proceeding by clause (i)), the relief sought by the Board pursuant to subparagraph (G) may be requested under the laws of such State.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Board may, as conservator or liquidating agent and for purposes of carrying out any power, authority, or duty with respect to an insured credit union (including determining any claim against the credit union and determining and realizing upon any asset of any person in the course of collecting money due the credit union), exercise any power established under , and the provisions of such section shall apply with respect to the exercise of any such power under this subparagraph in the same manner as such provisions apply under such section.section 1786(p) of this title

children.heading: Authority of Board
children.label: ii Authority of Board
children.text: A subpoena or subpoena duces tecum may be issued under clause (i) only by, or with the written approval of, the Board or their designees.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: iii Rule of construction
children.text: This subsection shall not be construed as limiting any rights that the Board, in any capacity, might otherwise have under .section 1786(p) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: exercise all powers and authorities specifically granted to conservators or liquidating agents, respectively, under this chapter and such incidental powers as shall be necessary to carry out such powers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: take any action authorized by this chapter,

children.heading: Exemption from criminal prosecution
children.label: K Exemption from criminal prosecution
children.text: The Administration shall be exempt from all prosecution by the United States or any State, county, municipality, or local authority for any criminal offense arising under Federal, State, county, municipal, or local law, which was allegedly committed by a credit union, or persons acting on behalf of a credit union, prior to the appointment of the Administration as liquidating agent.

children.heading: Authority of liquidating agent to determine claims
children.label: 3 Authority of liquidating agent to determine claims

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board may, as liquidating agent, determine claims in accordance with the requirements of this subsection and regulations prescribed under paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: promptly publish a notice to the credit union’s creditors to present their claims, together with proof, to the liquidating agent by a date specified in the notice which shall be not less than 90 days after the publication of such notice; and

children.label: ii
children.text: republish such notice approximately 1 month and 2 months, respectively, after the publication under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: at the creditor’s last address appearing in such books; or

children.label: ii
children.text: upon discovery of the name and address of a claimant not appearing on the credit union’s books within 30 days after the discovery of such name and address.

children.heading: Rulemaking authority relating to determination of claims
children.label: 4 Rulemaking authority relating to determination of claims
children.text: The Board may prescribe regulations regarding the allowance or disallowance of claims by the liquidating agent and providing for administrative determination of claims and review of such determination.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Before the end of the 180-day period beginning on the date any claim against a credit union is filed with the Board as liquidating agent, the Board shall determine whether to allow or disallow the claim and shall notify the claimant of any determination with respect to such claim.

children.heading: Extension of time
children.label: ii Extension of time
children.text: The period described in clause (i) may be extended by a written agreement between the claimant and the Board.

children.label: I
children.text: on the credit union’s books;

children.label: II
children.text: in the claim filed by the claimant; or

children.label: III
children.text: in documents submitted in proof of the claim.

children.heading: Contents of notice of disallowance
children.label: iv Contents of notice of disallowance
I
children.text: a statement of each reason for the disallowance; and

children.label: II
children.text: the procedures available for obtaining agency review of the determination to disallow the claim or judicial determination of the claim.

children.heading: Allowance of proven claims
children.label: B Allowance of proven claims
children.text: The liquidating agent shall allow any claim received on or before the date specified in the notice published under paragraph (3)(B)(i) by the liquidating agent from any claimant which is proved to the satisfaction of the liquidating agent.

children.heading: Disallowance of claims filed after end of filing period
children.label: C Disallowance of claims filed after end of filing period

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), claims filed after the date specified in the notice published under paragraph (3)(B)(i) shall be disallowed and such disallowance shall be final.

children.label: I
children.text: the claimant did not receive notice of the appointment of the liquidating agent in time to file such claim before such date; and

children.label: II
children.text: such claim is filed in time to permit payment of such claim.

children.heading: Authority to disallow claims
children.label: D Authority to disallow claims
children.text: The liquidating agent may disallow any portion of any claim by a creditor or claim of security, preference, or priority which is not proved to the satisfaction of the liquidating agent.

children.heading: No judicial review of determination pursuant to subparagraph (D)
children.label: E No judicial review of determination pursuant to subparagraph (D)
children.text: No court may review the Board’s determination pursuant to subparagraph (D) to disallow a claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitation tolled
children.label: i Statute of limitation tolled
children.text: For purposes of any applicable statute of limitations, the filing of a claim with the liquidating agent shall constitute a commencement of an action.

children.heading: No prejudice to other actions
children.label: ii No prejudice to other actions
children.text: Subject to paragraph (12), the filing of a claim with the liquidating agent shall not prejudice any right of the claimant to continue any action which was filed before the appointment of the liquidating agent.

children.heading: Provision for agency review or judicial determination of claims
children.label: 6 Provision for agency review or judicial determination of claims
i
children.text: the end of the period described in paragraph (5)(A)(i) with respect to any claim against a credit union for which the Board is liquidating agent; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of any notice of disallowance of such claim pursuant to paragraph (5)(A)(i),

children.label: i
children.text: request administrative review of any claim in accordance with subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (7); or

children.label: ii
children.text: file suit on such claim (or continue an action commenced before the appointment of the liquidating agent),

children.heading: Administrative hearing
children.label: A Administrative hearing
children.text: If any claimant requests review under this subparagraph in lieu of filing or continuing any action under paragraph (6) and the Board agrees to such request, the Board shall consider the claim after opportunity for a hearing on the record. The final determination of the Board with respect to such claim shall be subject to judicial review under chapter 7 of title 5.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Board shall also establish such alternative dispute resolution processes as may be appropriate for the resolution of claims filed under paragraph (5)(A)(i).

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: ii Criteria
children.text: In establishing alternative dispute resolution processes, the Board shall strive for procedures which are expeditious, fair, independent, and low cost.

children.heading: Voluntary binding or nonbinding procedures
children.label: iii Voluntary binding or nonbinding procedures
children.text: The Board may establish both binding and nonbinding processes, which may be conducted by any government or private party, but all parties, including the claimant and the Board, must agree to the use of the process in a particular case.

children.heading: Consideration of incentives
children.label: iv Consideration of incentives
children.text: The Board shall seek to develop incentives for claimants to participate in the alternative dispute resolution process.

children.label: i
children.text: allege the existence of legally valid and enforceable or perfected security interests in assets of any credit union for which the Board has been appointed liquidating agent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: allege that irreparable injury will occur if the routine claims procedure is followed.

children.label: I
children.text: whether to allow or disallow such claim; or

children.label: II
children.text: whether such claim should be determined pursuant to the procedures established pursuant to paragraph (5); or

children.label: ii
children.text: notify the claimant of the determination, and if the claim is disallowed, a statement of each reason for the disallowance and the procedure for obtaining agency review or judicial determination.

children.heading: Period for filing or renewing suit
children.label: C Period for filing or renewing suit
i
children.text: the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date of the filing of a request for expedited relief; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date the Board denies the claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: D Statute of limitations
children.text: If an action described in subparagraph (C) is not filed, or the motion to renew a previously filed suit is not made, before the end of the 30-day period beginning on the date on which such action or motion may be filed in accordance with subparagraph (B), the claim shall be deemed to be disallowed as of the end of such period (other than any portion of such claim which was allowed by the liquidating agent), such disallowance shall be final, and the claimant shall have no further rights or remedies with respect to such claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitation tolled
children.label: i Statute of limitation tolled
children.text: For purposes of any applicable statute of limitations, the filing of a claim with the liquidating agent shall constitute a commencement of an action.

children.heading: No prejudice to other actions
children.label: ii No prejudice to other actions
children.text: Subject to paragraph (12), the filing of a claim with the liquidating agent shall not prejudice any right of the claimant to continue any action which was filed before the appointment of the liquidating agent.

children.heading: Requirements
children.label: A Requirements
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), any agreement which does not meet the requirements set forth in shall not form the basis of, or substantially comprise, a claim against the liquidating agent or the Board.section 1788(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: Exception to contemporaneous execution requirement
children.label: B Exception to contemporaneous execution requirement
children.text: Notwithstanding , any agreement between a Federal home loan bank or Federal Reserve bank and any insured credit union which was executed before the extension of credit by such bank to such credit union shall be treated as having been executed contemporaneously with such extension of credit for purposes of subparagraph (A).section 1788(a)(3) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The liquidating agent may, in the liquidating agent’s discretion and to the extent funds are available, pay creditor claims which are allowed by the liquidating agent, approved by the Board pursuant to a final determination pursuant to paragraph (7) or (8), or determined by the final judgment of any court of competent jurisdiction in such manner and amounts as are authorized under this chapter.

children.heading: Payment of dividends on claims
children.label: B Payment of dividends on claims
children.text: The liquidating agent may, in the liquidating agent’s sole discretion, pay dividends on proved claims at any time, and no liability shall attach to the Board (in such Board’s corporate capacity or as liquidating agent), by reason of any such payment, for failure to pay dividends to a claimant whose claim is not proved at the time of any such payment.

children.heading: Subrogated claims; claims of uninsured accountholders and other creditors
children.label: A Subrogated claims; claims of uninsured accountholders and other creditors
i
children.text: retain for the account of the Board such portion of the amounts realized from any liquidation as the Board may be entitled to receive in connection with the subrogation of the claims of accountholders; and

children.label: ii
children.text: pay to accountholders and other creditors the net amounts available for distribution to them.

children.heading: Distribution to shareholders of amounts remaining after payment of all other claims and expenses
children.label: B Distribution to shareholders of amounts remaining after payment of all other claims and expenses
children.text: In any case in which funds remain after all accountholders, creditors, other claimants, and administrative expenses are paid, the liquidating agent shall distribute such funds to the credit union’s shareholders or members together with the accounting report required under paragraph (14)(C).

children.label: i
children.text: 45 days, in the case of any conservator; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 90 days, in the case of any liquidating agent,

children.heading: Grant of stay by all courts required
children.label: B Grant of stay by all courts required
children.text: Upon receipt of a request by any conservator or liquidating agent pursuant to subparagraph (A) for a stay of any judicial action or proceeding in any court with jurisdiction of such action or proceeding, the court shall grant such stay as to all parties.

children.heading: Prior final adjudication
children.label: A Prior final adjudication
children.text: The Board shall abide by any final unappealable judgment of any court of competent jurisdiction which was rendered before the appointment of the Board as conservator or liquidating agent.

children.heading: Rights and remedies of conservator or liquidating agent
children.label: B Rights and remedies of conservator or liquidating agent
i
children.text: have all the rights and remedies available to the credit union (before the appointment of such conservator or liquidating agent) and the Board in its corporate capacity, including removal to Federal court and all appellate rights; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not be required to post any bond in order to pursue such remedies.

children.heading: No attachment or execution
children.label: C No attachment or execution
children.text: No attachment or execution may issue by any court upon assets in the possession of the liquidating agent.

children.label: i
children.text: any claim or action for payment from, or any action seeking a determination of rights with respect to, the assets of any credit union for which the Board has been appointed liquidating agent, including assets which the Board may acquire from itself as such liquidating agent; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any claim relating to any act or omission of such credit union or the Board as liquidating agent.

children.heading: Statute of limitations for actions brought by conservator or liquidating agent
children.label: 14 Statute of limitations for actions brought by conservator or liquidating agent
I
children.text: the 6-year period beginning on the date the claim accrues; or

children.label: II
children.text: the period applicable under State law; and

children.label: I
children.text: the 3-year period beginning on the date the claim accrues; or

children.label: II
children.text: the period applicable under State law.

children.heading: Determination of the date on which a claim accrues
children.label: B Determination of the date on which a claim accrues
i
children.text: the date of the appointment of the Board as conservator or liquidating agent; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the cause of action accrues.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board as conservator or liquidating agent shall, consistent with the accounting and reporting practices and procedures established by the Board, maintain a full accounting of each conservatorship and liquidation or other disposition of credit unions in default.

children.heading: Annual accounting or report
children.label: B Annual accounting or report
children.text: With respect to each conservatorship or liquidation to which the Board was appointed, the Board shall make an annual accounting or report, as appropriate, available to the Comptroller General of the United States or, in the case of a State-chartered credit union, the authority which appointed the Board as conservator or liquidating agent.

children.heading: Availability of reports
children.label: C Availability of reports
children.text: Any report prepared pursuant to subparagraph (B) shall be made available by the Board upon request to any shareholder of the credit union for which the Board was appointed conservator or liquidating agent or any other member of the public.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), after the end of the 6-year period beginning on the date the Board is appointed as liquidating agent of an insured credit union, the Board may destroy any records of such credit union which the Board, in the Board’s discretion, determines to be unnecessary unless directed not to do so by a court of competent jurisdiction or governmental agency, or prohibited by law.

children.heading: Old records
children.label: ii Old records
children.text: Notwithstanding clause (i) the Board may destroy records of an insured credit union which are at least 10 years old as of the date on which the Board is appointed as liquidating agent of such credit union in accordance with clause (i) at any time after such appointment is final, without regard to the 6-year period of limitation contained in clause (i).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board, as conservator or liquidating agent for any insured credit union, may avoid a transfer of any interest of an institution-affiliated party, or any person who the Board determines is a debtor of the institution, in property, or any obligation incurred by such party or person, that was made within 5 years of the date on which the Board becomes conservator or liquidating agent if such party or person voluntarily or involuntarily made such transfer or incurred such liability with the intent to hinder, delay, or defraud the insured credit union or the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: the initial transferee of such transfer or the institution-affiliated party or person for whose benefit such transfer was made; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any immediate or mediate transferee of any such initial transferee.

children.heading: Rights of transferee or obligee
children.label: C Rights of transferee or obligee
i
children.text: any transferee that takes for value, including satisfaction or securing of a present or antecedent debt, in good faith; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any immediate or mediate good faith transferee of such transferee.

children.heading: Rights under this paragraph
children.label: D Rights under this paragraph
children.text: The rights of the Board under this paragraph shall be superior to any rights of a trustee or any other party (other than any party which is a Federal agency) under title 11.

children.heading: Provisions relating to contracts entered into before appointment of conservator or liquidating agent
children.label: c Provisions relating to contracts entered into before appointment of conservator or liquidating agent
A
children.text: to which such credit union is a party;

children.label: B
children.text: the performance of which the conservator or liquidating agent, in the conservator’s or liquidating agent’s discretion, determines to be burdensome; and

children.label: C
children.text: the disaffirmance or repudiation of which the conservator or liquidating agent determines, in the conservator’s or liquidating agent’s discretion, will promote the orderly administration of the credit union’s affairs.

children.heading: Timing of repudiation
children.label: 2 Timing of repudiation
children.text: The conservator or liquidating agent appointed for any insured credit union shall determine whether or not to exercise the rights of repudiation under this subsection within a reasonable period following such appointment.

children.label: i
children.text: limited to actual direct compensatory damages; and

children.label: I
children.text: the date of the appointment of the conservator or liquidating agent; or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any contract or agreement referred to in paragraph (8), the date of the disaffirmance or repudiation of such contract or agreement.

children.heading: No liability for other damages
children.label: B No liability for other damages
i
children.text: punitive or exemplary damages;

children.label: ii
children.text: damages for lost profits or opportunity; or

children.label: iii
children.text: damages for pain and suffering.

children.heading: Measure of damages for repudiation of financial contracts
children.label: C Measure of damages for repudiation of financial contracts
i
children.text: deemed to include normal and reasonable costs of cover or other reasonable measures of damages utilized in the industries for such contract and agreement claims; and

children.label: ii
children.text: paid in accordance with this subsection and subsection (f) except as otherwise specifically provided in this section.

children.heading: Leases under which the credit union is the lessee
children.label: 4 Leases under which the credit union is the lessee

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the conservator or liquidating agent disaffirms or repudiates a lease under which the credit union was the lessee, the conservator or liquidating agent shall not be liable for any damages (other than damages determined pursuant to subparagraph (B)) for the disaffirmance or repudiation of such lease.

children.label: I
children.text: the notice of disaffirmance or repudiation is mailed; or

children.label: II
children.text: the disaffirmance or repudiation becomes effective,

children.label: ii
children.text: have no claim for damages under any acceleration clause or other penalty provision in the lease; and

children.label: iii
children.text: have a claim for any unpaid rent, subject to all appropriate offsets and defenses, due as of the date of the appointment which shall be paid in accordance with this subsection and subsection (b).

children.heading: Leases under which the credit union is the lessor
children.label: 5 Leases under which the credit union is the lessor
i
children.text: treat the lease as terminated by such repudiation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: remain in possession of the leasehold interest for the balance of the term of the lease unless the lessee defaults under the terms of the lease after the date of such repudiation.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to lessee remaining in possession
children.label: B Provisions applicable to lessee remaining in possession
I
children.text: shall continue to pay the contractual rent pursuant to the terms of the lease after the date of the repudiation of such lease; and

children.label: II
children.text: may offset against any rent payment which accrues after the date of the repudiation of the lease, any damages which accrue after such date due to the nonperformance of any obligation of the credit union under the lease after such date; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the conservator or liquidating agent shall not be liable to the lessee for any damages arising after such date as a result of the repudiation other than the amount of any offset allowed under clause (i)(II).

children.heading: Contracts for the sale of real property
children.label: 6 Contracts for the sale of real property
i
children.text: treat the contract as terminated by such repudiation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: remain in possession of such real property.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to purchaser remaining in possession
children.label: B Provisions applicable to purchaser remaining in possession
I
children.text: shall continue to make all payments due under the contract after the date of the repudiation of the contract; and

children.label: II
children.text: may offset against any such payments any damages which accrue after such date due to the nonperformance (after such date) of any obligation of the credit union under the contract; and

children.label: I
children.text: not be liable to the purchaser for any damages arising after such date as a result of the repudiation other than the amount of any offset allowed under clause (i)(II);

children.label: II
children.text: deliver title to the purchaser in accordance with the provisions of the contract; and

children.label: III
children.text: have no obligation under the contract other than the performance required under subclause (II).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No provision of this paragraph shall be construed as limiting the right of the conservator or liquidating agent to assign the contract described in subparagraph (A) and sell the property subject to the contract and the provisions of this paragraph.

children.heading: No liability after assignment and sale
children.label: ii No liability after assignment and sale
children.text: If an assignment and sale described in clause (i) is consummated, the conservator or liquidating agent shall have no further liability under the contract described in subparagraph (A) or with respect to the real property which was the subject of such contract.

children.label: i
children.text: a claim to be paid in accordance with subsection (b); and

children.label: ii
children.text: deemed to have arisen as of the date the conservator or liquidating agent was appointed.

children.heading: Services performed after appointment and prior to repudiation
children.label: B Services performed after appointment and prior to repudiation
i
children.text: the other party shall be paid under the terms of the contract for the services performed; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of such payment shall be treated as an administrative expense of the conservatorship or liquidation.

children.heading: Acceptance of performance no bar to subsequent repudiation
children.label: C Acceptance of performance no bar to subsequent repudiation
children.text: The acceptance by any conservator or liquidating agent of services referred to in subparagraph (B) in connection with a contract described in such subparagraph shall not affect the right of the conservator or liquidating agent to repudiate such contract under this section at any time after such performance.

children.heading: Rights of parties to contracts
children.label: A Rights of parties to contracts
i
children.text: any right such person has to cause the termination, liquidation, or acceleration of any qualified financial contract with an insured credit union which arises upon the appointment of the Board as liquidating agent for such credit union at any time after such appointment;

children.label: ii
children.text: any right under any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to 1 or more qualified financial contracts described in clause (i);1So in original. Probably should be followed by “or”.1

children.label: iii
children.text: any right to offset or net out any termination value, payment amount, or other transfer obligation arising under or in connection with 1 or more contracts and agreements described in clause (i), including any master agreement for such contracts or agreements.

children.heading: Applicability of other provisions
children.label: B Applicability of other provisions
children.text: Subsection (b)(12) shall apply in the case of any judicial action or proceeding brought against any liquidating agent referred to in subparagraph (A), or the credit union for which such liquidating agent was appointed, by any party to a contract or agreement described in subparagraph (A)(i) with such credit union.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (11), or any other Federal or State law relating to the avoidance of preferential or fraudulent transfers, the Board, whether acting as such or as conservator or liquidating agent of an insured credit union, may not avoid any transfer of money or other property in connection with any qualified financial contract with an insured credit union.section 91 of this title

children.heading: Exception for certain transfers
children.label: ii Exception for certain transfers
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any transfer of money or other property in connection with any qualified financial contract with an insured credit union if the Board determines that the transferee had actual intent to hinder, delay, or defraud such credit union, the creditors of such credit union, or any conservator or liquidating agent appointed for such credit union.

children.heading: Certain contracts and agreements defined
children.label: D Certain contracts and agreements defined

children.heading: Qualified financial contract
children.label: i Qualified financial contract
children.text: The term “qualified financial contract” means any securities contract, forward contract, repurchase agreement, and any similar agreement that the Board determines by regulation, resolution, or order to be a qualified financial contract for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: means a contract for the purchase, sale, or loan of a security, a certificate of deposit, a mortgage loan, any interest in a mortgage loan, a group or index of securities, certificates of deposit, or mortgage loans or interests therein (including any interest therein or based on the value thereof) or any option on any of the foregoing, including any option to purchase or sell any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option, and including any repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction on any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option (whether or not such repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction is a “repurchase agreement”, as defined in clause (v));

children.label: II
children.text: does not include any purchase, sale, or repurchase obligation under a participation in a commercial mortgage loan unless the Board determines by regulation, resolution, or order to include any such agreement within the meaning of such term;

children.label: III
children.text: means any option entered into on a national securities exchange relating to foreign currencies;

children.label: IV
children.text: means the guarantee (including by novation) by or to any securities clearing agency of any settlement of cash, securities, certificates of deposit, mortgage loans or interests therein, group or index of securities, certificates of deposit, or mortgage loans or interests therein (including any interest therein or based on the value thereof) or option on any of the foregoing, including any option to purchase or sell any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option (whether or not such settlement is in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in subclauses (I) through (XII) (other than subclause (II));

children.label: V
children.text: means any margin loan;

children.label: VI
children.text: means any extension of credit for the clearance or settlement of securities transactions;

children.label: VII
children.text: means any loan transaction coupled with a securities collar transaction, any prepaid securities forward transaction, or any total return swap transaction coupled with a securities sale transaction;

children.label: VIII
children.text: means any other agreement or transaction that is similar to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: IX
children.text: means any combination of the agreements or transactions referred to in this clause;

children.label: X
children.text: means any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: XI
children.text: means a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), (VIII), (IX), or (X), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a securities contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a securities contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), (VIII), (IX), or (X); and

children.label: XII
children.text: means any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause, including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause.

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to a futures commission merchant, a contract for the purchase or sale of a commodity for future delivery on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade;

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to a foreign futures commission merchant, a foreign future;

children.label: III
children.text: with respect to a leverage transaction merchant, a leverage transaction;

children.label: IV
children.text: with respect to a clearing organization, a contract for the purchase or sale of a commodity for future delivery on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade that is cleared by such clearing organization, or commodity option traded on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade that is cleared by such clearing organization;

children.label: V
children.text: with respect to a commodity options dealer, a commodity option;

children.label: VI
children.text: any other agreement or transaction that is similar to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: VII
children.text: any combination of the agreements or transactions referred to in this clause;

children.label: VIII

children.label: IX
children.text: a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), or (VIII), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a commodity contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a commodity contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), or (VIII); or

children.label: X
children.text: any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause, including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause.

children.text: a contract (other than a commodity contract) for the purchase, sale, or transfer of a commodity or any similar good, article, service, right, or interest which is presently or in the future becomes the subject of dealing in the forward contract trade, or product or byproduct thereof, with a maturity date more than 2 days after the date the contract is entered into, including, a repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction (whether or not such repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction is a “repurchase agreement”, as defined in clause (v)), consignment, lease, swap, hedge transaction, deposit, loan, option, allocated transaction, unallocated transaction, or any other similar agreement;2So in original. The comma probably should not appear.2

children.label: II
children.text: any combination of agreements or transactions referred to in subclauses (I) and (III);

children.label: III

children.label: IV
children.text: a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclauses (I), (II), or (III), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a forward contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a forward contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (II), or (III); or

children.label: V
children.text: any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), or (IV), including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in any such subclause.

children.label: I
children.text: means an agreement, including related terms, which provides for the transfer of one or more certificates of deposit, mortgage-related securities (as such term is defined in the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 [ et seq.]), mortgage loans, interests in mortgage-related securities or mortgage loans, eligible bankers’ acceptances, qualified foreign government securities or securities that are direct obligations of, or that are fully guaranteed by, the United States or any agency of the United States against the transfer of funds by the transferee of such certificates of deposit, eligible bankers’ acceptances, securities, mortgage loans, or interests with a simultaneous agreement by such transferee to transfer to the transferor thereof certificates of deposit, eligible bankers’ acceptances, securities, mortgage loans, or interests as described above, at a date certain not later than 1 year after such transfers or on demand, against the transfer of funds, or any other similar agreement;15 U.S.C. 78a
